constructs.name: nesting_clause_phrase
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=Ellp [phrase function=Conj]] GO
constructs.teaches: object blocks nest; inner matches within the outer's monads

constructs.name: sequence_two_phrases
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause [phrase function=Conj][phrase function=Objc]] GO
constructs.teaches: bare juxtaposition means STRICTLY ADJACENT — second phrase begins at the monad right after the first ends; no gap allowed

constructs.name: sequence_gap_dotdot
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause [phrase function=Conj] .. [phrase function=Objc]] GO
constructs.teaches: .. (power block) allows arbitrary space between blocks inside the parent; contrast with bare juxtaposition which forces adjacency

constructs.name: first_phrase
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause [phrase first function=Conj]] GO
constructs.teaches: first anchors a block to the start of its parent — the phrase must begin at the clause's first monad

constructs.name: last_phrase
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause [phrase last function=Pred]] GO
constructs.teaches: last anchors a block to the end of its parent — the phrase must end at the clause's last monad

constructs.name: first_and_dotdot
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=Ellp [phrase first function=Conj] .. [phrase function=Objc]] GO
constructs.teaches: canonical 'starts with X, followed somewhere by Y': first pins the conjunction to the clause start; .. lets the object phrase appear anywhere after inside the clause

constructs.name: focus_inner_block
constructs.mql: SELECT FOCUS OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=Ellp [phrase focus function=Objc]] GO
constructs.teaches: SELECT FOCUS OBJECTS + focus keyword on a block: the sheaf returns only the focus-marked objects (phrases here), not the outer clause; structural context is a constraint, not a result

constructs.name: notexist_phrase
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=Ellp NOTEXIST [phrase function=Objc]] GO
constructs.teaches: NOTEXIST turns an inner block into a negative constraint — ellipsis clauses that contain NO object phrase

constructs.name: boolean_or_in_word
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [word sp=verb OR sp=adjv] GO
constructs.teaches: OR in feature constraints; default precedence: AND binds tighter than OR, so parentheses can be added when needed to override (e.g. sp=verb AND (vs=nif OR vs=piel) requires parens so OR applies to both vs comparisons)

constructs.name: boolean_not_in_word
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [word sp=verb AND NOT vt=ptca] GO
constructs.teaches: NOT in feature constraints; NOT binds tightest (tighter than AND)

constructs.name: in_set_phrase_function
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [phrase function IN (Objc, PreO, PtcO)] GO
constructs.teaches: IN (list) for set membership — matches any phrase whose function is one of the listed enum values

constructs.name: comparison_integer
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [verse verse > 10] GO
constructs.teaches: integer comparison with > operator; verse number is an integer feature on the verse object type

constructs.name: string_equality_lex
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [word lex='BR>['] GO
constructs.teaches: string features compare QUOTED (single quotes); lex is a string feature; verbs carry a trailing [ in BHSA transliteration

constructs.name: object_reference_agreement
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [phrase typ=NP [word AS w1 sp=subs]] .. [phrase typ=AdjP [word sp=adjv AND nu IN w1.nu AND gn IN w1.gn]] GO
constructs.teaches: AS <name> binds a matched object; <name>.<feature> in a later block (via IN form) compares against that bound object's feature — used for agreement (number, gender) between a noun and an adjective

constructs.name: get_features_on_word
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [word sp=verb AND vs=nif GET lex, gloss, g_word_utf8] GO
constructs.teaches: per-block GET lists the features to return for matched objects; GET is per-block not per-statement

constructs.name: get_features_nested
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=Ellp GET typ [phrase function=Objc GET function [word sp=subs GET lex, gloss]]] GO
constructs.teaches: each block in a nested query can carry its own GET; features come back attached to the matched object at that level in the result sheaf

constructs.name: probe_clause_type
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause typ=NmCl] GO
constructs.teaches: cheap object-type probe for clause with a typ filter; confirms nominal clauses exist in the database

constructs.name: probe_clause_atom_type
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [clause_atom typ=Ellp] GO
constructs.teaches: clause_atom object type probe with typ filter; clause_atom is the subunit of a clause (coordinated or subordinated portion)

constructs.name: probe_phrase_atom_function
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [phrase_atom function=Pred] GO
constructs.teaches: phrase_atom object type probe with function filter; phrase_atom is the subunit of a phrase

constructs.name: probe_word_sp
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [word sp=nmpr] GO
constructs.teaches: word-level object probe filtering by part of speech; nmpr = proper noun

constructs.name: probe_sentence_nested_verb
constructs.mql: SELECT ALL OBJECTS WHERE [sentence [word sp=verb AND vt=impv]] GO
constructs.teaches: sentence object type probe with nested word constraint; skipping levels ([sentence [word]]) is legal — inner need not be a direct child
